# jk context

Manage Jenkins contexts.

Environment variable JK_CONTEXT can be used instead of the --context flag.
Precedence: --context flag > JK_CONTEXT env var > active context in config

```
jk context <command> [flags]
```

## Subcommands

| Subcommand | Description | Key Flags |
|---|---|---|
| [ls](#jk-context-ls) | List configured contexts | — |
| [rm](#jk-context-rm) | Remove a context and its credentials | — |
| [use](#jk-context-use) | Set the active context | — |

## jk context ls

List configured contexts

### Usage

```
jk context ls [flags]
```

### Inherited Flags

| Flag | Short | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `--context` | `-c` | Active Jenkins context name (or set JK_CONTEXT env var) |
| `--format` |  | Output format: json, yaml |
| `--jq` |  | Filter JSON output using jq expression (requires --json or --format=json) |
| `--json` |  | Output in JSON format when supported |
| `--quiet` | `-q` | Suppress non-essential output |
| `--template` | `-t` | Format output using Go template (requires --json or --format=json) |
| `--yaml` |  | Output in YAML format when supported |

## jk context rm

```

```

| `--format` |  | Output format: json, yaml |

| `--template` | `-t` | Format output using Go template (requires --json or --format=json) |
| `--yaml` |  | Output in YAML format when supported |

### Usage

```
jk context use <name> [flags]
```

| Flag | Short | Description |
|---|---|---|

| `--template` | `-t` | Format output using Go template (requires --json or --format=json) |
